Insights
Latest from D&AD
Legends of Design: Aubrey Powell tells the story behind some of the most iconic album covers of the 20th century

Watch a live conversation about the creative partnership, tenacity and experimentation that powered design group Hipgnosis during its heyday in the 1960s and 70s

How Mulberry’s in-house marketing team keeps creativity alive while boosting sales

D&AD Luxury Jury President and Mulberry Brand Director Priya Matadeen explains how her in-house team balances creative excellence with ROIs and efficiency

Oscar season: Looking back at D&AD Award-winning work made by Academy Award winners

Standout work made by creatives who have won two of the world’s toughest-to-win accolades

Three ways to keep creativity in craft alive

Three takeaways from a panel at D&AD HQ in London, where industry luminaries discussed a pertinent question – is creativity in craft dead?

Super Bowl Sunday: D&AD Sports Entertainment judge Jonathan Santana on his newest commercial for Volkswagen and his favourite spots of all time

Johannes Leonardo’s CCO explains how he reimagined “Drivers wanted’ for a new generation

“I draw people sitting next to me on the London underground” – D&AD’s Illustration Jury President Noma Bar on where he finds inspiration

"The Handmaid's Tale" illustrator explains how he found his distinctive style

Why taste will become the rarest and most coveted commodity

There’s been a huge shift in branding - Cat How, D&AD’s New Brand Identity Jury President for 2026, gives three propositions to explain the reasons why

“We’re living through one of the most thrilling creative shifts since the dawn of digital” – Brand Identity Refresh Jury President Teemu Suviala

Landor’s Global Chief Creative Officer on why he thinks creativity is just getting started and what you can do to spark yours

“Creativity thrives where people are given a chance to be seen and heard” – Casting Jury President Claire Catterson

The Channel 4 Idents Casting Director on why keeping creativity alive and thriving means fostering diversity and inclusion

Snap’s Resh Sidhu on how the creative frontier has shifted

If you only search for creativity in familiar places, you might miss it – so where should you be looking for it now?

“They’ll be bored of the slop soon enough” – Writing for Design Jury President Holly Kielty

Writer and creative strategist Holly Kielty explains why we should view creativity as an instinctive behaviour rather than just a result

The Glue Society’s Jonathan Kneebone explores creativity in films Goldfinger and Once Upon a Time in the West

Jonathan Kneebone on one of the earliest Black Pencils in D&AD’s history

关于.text:
Consent
Details
Ad Settings
About
Responsible use of your data
We and our 1022 partners process your personal data, e.g. your IP-number, using technology such as cookies to store and access information on your device in order to serve personalized ads and content, ad and content measurement, audience research and services development. You have a choice in who uses your data and for what purposes. Your privacy choices are only applicable on this digital property where you have made your choices. You can change or withdraw your consent any time from the Cookie Declaration or by clicking on the Privacy trigger icon.

If you allow, we would also like to:
Collect information about your geographical location which can be accurate to within several meters
Identify your device by actively scanning it for specific characteristics (fingerprinting)
Find out more about how your personal data is processed and set your preferences in the details section.

We use cookies to personalise content and ads, to provide social media features and to analyse our traffic. We also share information about your use of our site with our social media, advertising and analytics partners who may combine it with other information that you’ve provided to them or that they’ve collected from your use of their services.
